programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A doctor dies on board the USS Borealis during a heavy storm and when the ship is brought into Norfolk Naval Yard, Ducky's investigation reveals a telltale mark on the victim's carotid artery, suggesting he was injected with poison. Initial inquiries focus on a petty officer with a history of drug use who may have wanted the doctor to remain silent about his problems. American drama, starring David McCallum

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team are called in when a Navy reservist returns home to find her husband missing and her living room covered in blood, and make a grim discovery when they find a finger in the bin. Meanwhile, Ziva ruffles a few feathers by insisting on continuing her investigation into the murder of her father, asking for permission to go to Rome on the trail of the chief suspect. Mark Harmon stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: New Mossad director Orli Elbaz asks for the team's help in finding Ilan Bodnar when one of her agents is shot dead in Virginia, telling Gibbs he was investigating several operatives who had gone rogue after Eli David's death. Ziva travels with Tony to Berlin in her pursuit of Bodnar, the man she believes was responsible for the death of her father. Guest starring Marina Sirtis (Star Trek: The Next Generation)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team tracks Bodnar's SUV to a remote house in the countryside, but inside find only his dead accomplice - identified as a South African mercenary who helped Bodnar procure diamonds. Ziva and Tony, meanwhile, discharge themselves from hospital and wheedle information out of Abby to continue investigating on the quiet, having been warned by Homeland Security to stay off the case

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A cook from a US Navy destroyer tells McGee and Gibbs he believes someone is stalking him with the intention of trying to make him poison the crew, and a picture on a stolen phone leads the team to a group testing the psychological effects of surveillance. Meanwhile, Richard Parsons from the Inspector General's office closes in on Ziva as he investigates the death of Ilan Bodnar, the man who killed her father. Guest starring Colin Hanks (Gus Grimly in the Fargo TV series)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team must save Cabe and his protege Tim when they are captured during a secret mission in Djibouti, while Sylvester hires a lawyer to help him deal with his auditions contracts

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team's mission to recover the remains of fallen Marines in Vietnam is threatened, along with their lives, when a deadly tornado heads directly towards them

programme.title: CSI: Cyber
programme.desc: A new code exploits a printer's design flaw, allowing cyber-arsonists to set fires remotely by using wi-fi data connections, and Brody gets a blast from the past when he discovers the person responsible. Meanwhile, Elijah has personal problems when his ex-wife decides to move to San Diego with their daughter. Patricia Arquette stars

programme.title: CSI: Cyber
programme.desc: Following an explosion at a cinema, Avery and her team go in search of a bomber who intends to expose the truth about society's over-reliance on technology. As the investigation progresses, Ramirez and Nelson find a website which features a video of the bombing along with a sinister explanation, and they face a race to catch the culprit before the next bomb goes off. Patricia Arquette and James Van Der Beek star

programme.title: CSI: Cyber
programme.desc: The decomposing body of a young woman is found in the bathroom of a New York hotel, having been undisturbed for three days. When Avery Ryan's and her team investigate, they discover the victim seemed to have been ordering room service three days after her death, and her messages to staff had released vicious malware that eradicated several hours of surveillance footage from the hotel's security cameras. The chain of evidence leads Ryan to a hacker thought to have been on-site at the time of the woman's death, but as Nelson and Ramirez continue digging for clues, it becomes clear this case is far from closed. Patricia Arquette stars

programme.title: CSI: Cyber
programme.desc: Avery Ryan's old friend Jordan Tan seeks her help in locating his missing teenage daughter Zoey. When the team looks into high-schooler's online history, they discover she had been the target of hacks, faked phone calls and social media posts, as well as a malicious website titled 'Kill Yourself Zoey Tan'. The spate of abuse bears all the hallmarks of a particularly nasty case of online bullying, but Zoey's digital footprint offers the investigators a lead in the form of online boyfriend Owen, who lives 300 miles away and seems to have invited her to visit. However, when the team ventures to Owen's home, they discover his invitation was just another facet of the hate campaign, and with Zoey's whereabout still unknown, serious concerns are raised about her wellbeing. Crime drama, starring Patricia Arquette, with guests Byron Mann and BooBoo Stewart

programme.title: CSI: Cyber
programme.desc: The team investigates the abductions of young women whose social media pages continue to be updated, and Avery helps Trish McCarthy cope with the death of her sister. Hi-tech drama, with Patricia Arquette and guest star Rosanna Arquette

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: The local rugby team goes to France for a match. Vic finds his uncle's war grave, Simon has a fling with a French businesswoman, and Anna treats a boy for epilepsy

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: Crabbe investigates a crooked takeaway whose staff he suspects of cooking up a burglary, but as he delves deeper into the case he finds an altogether more sinister operation. Crime drama, starring Richard Griffiths, Maggie Steed, Samantha Janus and Malcolm Sinclair

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: Fisher comes under suspicion following a tragic accident during a pheasant shoot on a country estate, and asks Crabbe to investigate the case. Starring Richard Griffiths, Malcolm Sinclair and Ingrid Lacey

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: Framed by long-time enemy Ducos for stealing Napoleon's treasures, Sharpe is abandoned by his wife Jane, and goes on the run from both the English and the French until he can prove his innocence. Period adventure, starring Sean Bean, Daragh O'Malley, Abigail Cruttenden and Cecile Paoli

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Sontarans activate their masterplan and begin to choke Earth, while Unit is left defenceless, with a traitor in its ranks. As interplanetary war edges closer, the Doctor considers making the ultimate sacrifice to keep Martha and Donna alive. Freema Agyeman and Christopher Ryan guest star

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Tardis lands on the war-torn distant planet of Messaline, where a sample of the Doctor's DNA is used to create his clone, a fiesty young woman determined to defend her new home. While Donna and Martha get to know the newcomer, their time-travelling friend remains distant, and worries he may no longer be the last of the Time Lords. Guest starring Georgia Moffett, whose real-life father Peter Davison played the Doctor in the early 1980s

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Doctor takes Donna to 1926 where they meet Agatha Christie - on the day she famously disappeared for 10 days. A series of murders straight out of the writer's books unfolds, and the Doctor's investigation leads to a battle with a giant wasp. Guest starring Felicity Jones and Felicity Kendal

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: When the beaten body of wealthy German businessman Gunter Schmeikel is discovered in the harbour, Gently and Bacchus are forced to consider whether a barman's anti-German sentiments are a motive for murder. However, the case takes a sinister turn when a key witness to Schmeikel's death is revealed to be a killer trained by Army Special Forces. Martin Shaw and Lee Ingleby star, with Tim Healy

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: Alzheimer sufferer Edward Alderston comes under suspicion of involvement when an ex-finance company high-flyer responsible for the loss of many elderly people's life savings is found floating in a swimming pool with a samurai sword in his back

programme.title: Miss Fisher's Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: Phryne heads off for a quiet holiday at the seaside holiday town of Queenscliff, confident she will not be disturbed by an investigation. However, it is not long she and Jack discover buried treasure, tales of pirate ghosts - and murder. Australian crime drama, starring Essie Davis and Nathan Page

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: American diplomat Ryan Reed is shot dead and the team arrives at the scene to find his body posed - sitting upright on a bench with his hand tucked inside his jacket. An argument quickly ensues between the police and the US embassy, which claims the murder falls under its jurisdiction, while the Lyell crew fights against the bureaucracy of the FBI, aided only by the US deputy chief of mission Matt Garcia. However, when he goes missing, Nikki is concerned he might be the next target

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Les Dennis guest stars as a frantic son whose lecherous 75-year-old father has gone missing. Meanwhile, Terry uses an interpreter to interrogate a Togolese worker accused of theft, only to realise he's being made a fool of, and Lewis ignores warnings to stay away from Holly

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Samantha and Lewis question a woman whose infant son has accidentally ingested heroin, and pressure her into revealing the name of her dealer. Zain tells Neil he has been working undercover, adding that they can't arrest Kristen as she thinks he is a bent cop and there are bigger fish to fry

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Zain is assigned to investigate a suspicious warehouse fire with Kezia, but instead slopes off for another clandestine meeting with Kristen. Learning she has been assaulted, he tracks down her attacker and exacts some very rough justice indeed. Meanwhile, June grows concerned for an increasingly troubled Roger - despite Lewis's assurances that there's nothing to worry about

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Roger encounters a young woman whose financial problems have driven her to the brink of suicide, while June looks forward to a fortnight in the Seychelles with Rod Jessop and Kezia gets to know Smithy better. Louis Drake complains of being attacked in Kristen Shaw's club and the uniform branch commandeer CCTV footage which reveals Zain as the 'heavy' responsible

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Eleanor is adjusting to life on her own after her father's wedding. She is engaged to Dr Marsham, but still unsure whether this is the right thing to do

programme.title: Wuthering Heights
programme.desc: First in a two-part adaptation of Emily Bronte's classic tale. In 1815, Mr Earnshaw returns home from Liverpool with orphan Heathcliff, who befriends his daughter Cathy but is resented by his son Hindley. Upon his father's death 10 years later, Hindley inherits Wuthering Heights and forces Heathcliff to live in the stables, while Cathy must choose between a life of comfort and her love for Heathcliff. Tom Hardy, Charlotte Riley, Andrew Lincoln and Sarah Lancashire star

programme.title: Wuthering Heights
programme.desc: Conclusion. Tormented by his love for Cathy, Heathcliff arrives at Thrushcross Grange the day after her wedding to Edgar. Tension mounts as the pair acknowledge their passion for one another, but when she falls pregnant with Edgar's child, Heathcliff leaves Gimmerton to marry Isabella. Tom Hardy, Charlotte Riley, Andrew Lincoln, Rosalind Halstead and Burn Gorman star in Emily Bronte's classic tale

programme.title: dinnerladies
programme.desc: Bren has a bad day - a convict escapes in the fog on his way to Strangeways, the blood donor van turns up at the factory, and Tony's holiday photos cause upset. Comedy, starring Victoria Wood and Andrew Dunn

programme.title: dinnerladies
programme.desc: The staff have a bet on Bren's chances of romance with Tony, but the hapless cook's past finally catches up with her and threatens to ruin any prospects of romance. Meanwhile, Stan is furious with Philippa for failing to save the bulbs from last year's Christmas tree lights. Comedy, written by and starring Victoria Wood, with Thelma Barlow, Andrew Dunn, Celia Imrie and Shobna Gulati

programme.title: dinnerladies
programme.desc: Twinkle and Stan spoil Petula's plans for a peaceful family Christmas, and a baby is found abandoned near the canteen. Bren's romance with Tony is ruined by the unexpected reappearance of her husband, and Anita goes away - but leaves her inflatable man behind. Festive special from 1999, starring Victoria Wood

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The sleuthing housewife investigates a spiritualist who appears to be blackmailing her customers, while Geoffrey falls in love, but his secret romance threatens to undermine Hetty's carefully laid trap - and nearly causes him to quit the agency

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Rumours spread around Poplar that a sailor with smallpox is hiding somewhere in town. Nurse Crane and Dr Turner try to track him down before he causes an outbreak. Lucille treats an expectant mother on her own, but soon feels out of her depth when she displays signs of a serious medical condition. Trixie goes to stay with her godmother, Violet and Fred organise a picnic for the parish, and Tom and Barbara return home

programme.title: Rebus
programme.desc: While Rebus looks into the apparent suicide of a man at an investment bank, Clarke investigates a stabbing on the seedy Westmills estate. The inspector learns his victim had links to a chemical company building a new plant in the area, as well as an Angolan village where children have been used unscrupulously in drug testing - but before his latest informant can pass on vital evidence, she too is silenced. Drama, starring Ken Stott and Claire Price

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: Part two of two. A massive police search leads to the body of a missing five-year-old, the victim of sexual assault and strangulation. All the evidence points to a local drunkard being the guilty party _ but as the case goes to trial it becomes clear that the outcome is by no means assured. Gripping crime drama by Lynda La Plante, starring David Hayman, Kate Buffery, Helen McCrory and Rhys Ifans

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: Jo Mills takes on the case of a worker killed on a building site, convinced that the managing director of the company should be held accountable for his neglect of health and safety legislation. However, the Lord Chancellor's department once again try to divert the course of justice to avoid embarrassment - and Deed's ex-wife is appointed counsel for the defence. Legal drama, starring Martin Shaw and Jenny Seagrove

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The sleuthing housewife investigates a spiritualist who appears to be blackmailing her customers, while Geoffrey falls in love, but his secret romance threatens to undermine Hetty's carefully laid trap - and nearly causes him to quit the agency

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Mark invites Arthur and Pauline out for an Indian meal. Sharon pays some surprise visits around the square. Ricky tries to get Bianca a job at the cafe

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Tony's family problems take their toll when he answers an urgent call from his father while he's on duty, earning a rebuke from Smithy and adding weight to Heaton's view that Sun Hill could get better value from its staff by getting rid of the expensive veterans and taking on inexperienced youngsters. Sam works with boyfriend Stuart on the case of an old lag stabbed soon after his release from prison, but she turns to old pal Phil Hunter when she discovers she's pregnant

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Michael is suspicious when Abra tries to prevent a convicted murderer receiving a liver transplant. Kyla can only stand by while other people make decisions regarding her son Max's medical treatment while in foster care. Elliot tries to avoid Lady Byrne following their kiss, but realises that it might not have been an accident after all

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Events take a tragic turn when Jeff threatens a man while on a call-out with Nikki, and the arrival of an attractive neurologist causes problems between Harry and Charlie. Colette tells Simon about the baby, but he does not want to know, and the arrival of a prostitute in A&E only complicates matters. Hospital drama, starring Kelly Harrison, Christopher Colquhoun and Elizabeth Carling

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Kaye Wragg joins the cast as PC Diane Noble, who makes a good start with the Sun Hill team when she and Smithy arrest a key figure in the crack house raid. Gina is less than impressed with Heaton's heavy-handed tactics, which leave an elderly witness fighting for her life. Sam suffers a miscarriage and realises from Stuart's reaction that he isn't ready for a long-term commitment

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil is summoned for a confrontation with Peggy, who slaps him. The council force Steve to close the café. David continues to buy Bianca's affection

programme.title: Doctor Finlay
programme.desc: The medic resolves to track down a mysterious man who callously ignored his request to call for an ambulance - and Finlay's obsession soon leads him into a macabre world. Drama, starring David Rintoul

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Cardale is thrown into a state of shock when Alice North is brutally attacked in her own home. The shock helps push Joanna back into the arms of her ex-lover Martin Henderson. Can the pillar of the community recover from this devastating intrusion?

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James discovers the downside of life as a vet when the post-mortem results for Lord Hulton's horse arrive, while Siegfried's younger brother Tristan comes back from college and James is introduced to Mrs Pumphrey and her dog Tricki Woo

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: Hester decides to call in the experts to deal with redecorating - but her choice is less than ideal. Comedy, starring Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tom's son Geoffrey summons the courage to walk out on his wife, and moves into Bayview instead - but after a few days of his boring company Diana hatches a plan to get rid of him

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Newlyweds Fanny and Leclerc are jailed by the Germans for flying around in a bed attached to a balloon. Rene is horrified when Edith resolves to rescue them, but her plans are thwarted by a loose bedknob. Second World War comedy, starring Gorden Kaye and Carmen Silvera

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: As Clegg and Truly prepare to face life without Compo, a letter arrives addressed to their late friend from his son Tom. Long-running comedy, starring Peter Sallis, Frank Thornton and Tom Owen

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Laura and Rosemary investigate the mysterious death of a blind professor caught up in a bitter battle to save his beloved allotment from an unscrupulous land developer. A heady blend of weedkiller, cannabis and high finance hangs in the air as the duo search for clues - and another body is turned up before they track down the villain. Drama, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Famous thriller author Frank O'Toole is found stabbed through the heart after taking an early morning swim in the ocean. Suspicion soon falls on the victim's wife Valerie, but with eyewitnesses confirming he was swimming alone, how could anyone have got from the house to the sea without being detected? Away from the investigation, Dwayne concentrates on finding a date for a voodoo love festival. Whodunit, guest starring Simon Callow, Imogen Stubbs, and Sian Gibson of Car Shame fame

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Jack announces he is quitting the team, but before Sandra, Brian and Gerry can question his reasons, shadowy Whitehall figure Stephen Fisher arrives with one of his secret cases - the unsolved murder of a woman dating back more than 160 years. With just 24 hours to solve it, will the sleuths find time to get to the bottom of Jack's shock decision and persuade him to stick around?

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: An American assassin is asked to come out of retirement to kill an Englishman, and Tom is put in an intolerable position when Christine reveals sensitive information about the clandestine operation. Against her wishes, he puts the team on the case to find the would-be culprit, but his colleagues begin to doubt his judgement and motives when the trail goes cold. Crime drama, starring Matthew Macfadyen and Megan Dodds

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The team flies out to Zambia to investigate the death of a female doctor who disappeared two weeks earlier. It transpires the woman was volunteering for a Mission and had believed a number of refugees died in suspicious circumstances. Leo and Nikki carry out a post-mortem, but their findings throw the case into disarray. Patrick Baladi and Robert Pugh guest star

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Footage of wildlife that inhabit remote islands, revealing the advantages and disadvantages of the creatures' habitat. Three-toed sloths in the Caribbean and nesting albatross enjoy a peaceful existence untroubled by predators, but other animals are not so fortunate. Marine iguanas in the Galapagos Islands are preyed on by racer snakes from birth, while penguins on the Antarctic island of Zavodovski risk being battered against the rocks by fierce waves

programme.title: Countryfile

programme.title: Little Mix The Search

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce shares unseen items from recent roadshows, from a set of designer jewellery to an ancient strong-box and some engraved glasses which might not be all that they seem. Also among the eclectic finds are brooches handed down from the royal family, a spy camera, a prop from a St Trinian's film and a hand-cut paper Valentine designed to win a hand in marriage

programme.title: Us
programme.desc: Despite a rocky start in Paris, the Petersen family's European tour continues in Amsterdam where it seems that nothing can prevent the family from getting into the holiday spirit, not even a sleazy hotel that might actually be a brothel. But an explosive fight at breakfast looks like it might be the end of the line for their trip

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including Sheffield United v Leeds United at Bramall Lane and Manchester City v Leicester City at Etihad Stadium. Leeds are making a long-awaited return to top-flight football and will be looking to emulate their local rivals, who finished in ninth place last term. The Citizens prevailed 3-1 when they met Leicester here last season, but their opponents pushed them for second place for much of the campaign before fading to fifth after the restart. Plus, Tottenham Hotspur v Newcastle United, West Ham United v Wolverhampton Wanderers and a round-up of yesterday's goals

programme.title: Squad Goals
programme.desc: The January transfer window sees two new arrivals at West Ham United, but a mysterious virus begins to take its grip across the squad when the team return after the Christmas break

programme.title: The Water Diviner (2014)
programme.desc: An Australian farmer's life is shattered during the First World War, when he receives news that his three sons have been declared missing in action during the battle of Gallipoli. In the aftermath of the conflict, he travels to Turkey in the hope of discovering the truth about their fate. Period drama, directed by and starring Russell Crowe, with Olga Kurylenko

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: The return of the documentary using bodycam and CCTV footage to tell the stories of emergency service workers assaulted in the line of duty. Police officers are doused in petrol and threatened with being set alight when a moped chase ends in chaos

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin follow the development progress of properties in Brompton in Kent, Workington in Cumbria and Great Barr in the West Midlands

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: The England Illegal Money Lending Team target a suspect loan shark and raid his home, and a convicted loan shark has a caravan seized in Wales

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: Investigators uncover a senior manager gambling with an NHS credit card, and tackle the cowards targeting vulnerable elderly women for their life savings

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Richard Madley and Stephanie Connell help two teams search for items in Brackley, Northamptonshire, while presenter Eric Knowles discovers a marvel of Victorian timekeeping

programme.title: The Indian Doctor
programme.desc: High-flying Delhi graduate Dr Prem Sharma and his wife Kamini move to the UK after being wooed by dreams of riches and glamour, only to find themselves residing in a small Welsh mining village. While Prem strikes up new friendships and settles into life in the Valleys, Kamini wants them to leave as soon as possible. Comedy drama set in the 1960s, starring Sanjeev Bhaskar and Ayesha Dharker

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers celebrate quality produce that needs to be cooked with the respect it deserves, as they continue their culinary journey through time. The duo argue that, while convenience food serves a purpose, only the best ingredients and perfect cooking can produce something truly delicious

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a couple of keen horticulturalists who have £450,000 to find a home in the Yorkshire Dales, and also visits RHS Garden Harlow Carr to meet Alan Titchmarsh

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers have a £5,500 budget to create a dramatic Balinese garden full of greenery and tropical plants for newlyweds Peter and Samantha in Cheltenham

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades oversees proceedings as the show's experts restore an 80-year-old banjo, a pair of 1930s rollerskates and a 19th-century wooden salad bowl

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat. Presented by Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The programme hears from whistleblowers working inside the Government's new coronavirus tracking system. They are so concerned about NHS Test and Trace that they are speaking out to reveal chaos, technical problems, confusion, wasted resources and a system that does not appear to them to be working. There are also interviews with members of local public health teams, who say they have largely been ignored by the Government in favour of the private companies hired to run the new centralised tracking system

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian hosts a business meeting at home, determined to impress a council colleague with his hospitality skills - only for Max to storm in making accusations and threats. Isaac is reeling after his meeting with the school about the drugs, leading to an altercation with Phil, and Mick is disturbed to find Frankie taking photos of Ollie. Kheerat tries to explain himself to Karen, and Rainie and Stuart say their farewells on the night before their wedding

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: After a heavy night partying, Alison and Mike wake to find their house in disarray and with the clock ticking until a potentially crucial business partner comes to view the venue, the couple attempt a mass clean up. The ghosts are all acting very strangely, and Alison struggles to piece together the events of the night before to figure out what happened to them

programme.title: Freddie Flintoff: Living with Bulimia
programme.desc: The former England cricketer faces up to his eating disorder of 20 years as he challenges the stigma around the condition in men. He explores how bulimia has played a part in the course of his life, discusses his own experience in detail and meets specialists and young men with eating disorders across the UK

programme.title: Stand Up for Live Comedy
programme.desc: Showcase for emerging talent, filmed in six different cities across the UK in outdoor locations with socially distanced audiences. The first edition is from Bristol, where local comedian Jayde Adams plays host to Mo Omar, Lauren Pattison and Tom Lucy

programme.title: Yung Filly's Celeb Lock-In
programme.desc: The host is joined by Wayne and Frankie Bridge on the game show, with the couple helping four Geordie households competing to win the grand prize

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Actor, writer and TV presenter Richard Ayoade takes over the reins for an extended 2019 edition of the satirical quiz, with Pointless co-host Richard Osman and comedian Kiri Pritchard-McLean joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the stories making the headlines

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood examine footage of strange wildlife, starting with trees covered in spider webs and bees that make blue, red, and green honey

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: The team builds a den for 11-year-old aspiring journalist Soibi, who is finding it hard to write up her reports in the small bedroom she shares with her three-year-old sister

programme.title: All Over the Place
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and friends compete in Willaston's World Worm Charming Championships.

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Three badge holders meet David Attenborough and Dippy the Dinosaur embarks on a tour

programme.title: Deadly Dinosaurs with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve goes on a mission to track down the deadliest of all dinosaur defenders. He meets the armoured tank that is Nodosaurus, tests whether an Iguanodon really has what it takes to beat a T-Rex, and - thanks to some mega engineering - unleashes an Ankylosaurus tail-swinging robot that goes on the rampage

programme.title: Wonders of the Universe
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox continues his exploration of the origins of mankind by investigating the matter from which everything on Earth is formed. At a Hindu cremation in Nepal, he discusses the faith's philosophy of life as an eternal cycle of creation and destruction, and draws a parallel with the lives of the universe's stars - before revealing why everything on Earth was once a part of something else

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: On the Stoer Peninsula, Helen and Graham prepare for a visit from the vet, who is coming to take blood samples from the herd to test for a chronic wasting disease. In Northumberland, Emma's six-month-old puppy Grey is introduced to sheep for the first time, while in the Lake District, Matt and Dani Blair are dealing with an unexpected new arrival. On Little Rowater Farm in Aberdeenshire, a deer calf shows signs of being bullied by the herd

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: In the vast wilderness of Canada's west coast lie forests that are home to North American black bears that through a quirk of genetics are born pure white and known as ghost bears. Canadian film-maker Jeff Turner and his own family spend a year following a she-bear and its cubs as they coped with the constant threat of hunger and potential predators all around

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: Actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from TV chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo

programme.title: Live T20 Cricket
programme.desc: Isa Guha presents all the action from the third match of the five-game series, held at Incora County Ground in Derby. The tourists will be looking for revenge in this series after England beat them by 46 runs to reach the T20 World Cup semi-finals in March

programme.title: Cycling
programme.desc: Action from a recent event

programme.title: Live Women's FA Cup Football
programme.desc: Arsenal v Tottenham Hotspur (Kick-off 5.15pm). Coverage of the quarter-final between the London rivals, which takes place at Meadow Park in Boreham Wood. Arsenal have won this competition a record five times and will start this match as favourites. However, Spurs made a big statement this month when they signed American star Alex Morgan and will be looking to prove they are now a match for their neighbours

programme.title: Bernard Haitink, the Enigmatic Maestro
programme.desc: After conducting for 65 years, Bernard Haitink retired at the age of 90 in September 2019. The musicians he has worked with are puzzled by the secrets of his technique. He himself says his job is to embrace the orchestra without suffocating. Here, documentary-maker John Bridcut follows the Dutch maestro in his final year of music-making, watching him at work and talking to those who have worked alongside him throughout his long career

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig hosts the high-brow quiz, serving up a selection of questions on the subject of restaurants. Trying to come up with the answers are Alan Davies, Phill Jupitus, Sara Pascoe and Mark Watson

programme.title: McQueen (2018)
programme.desc: Documentary charting the life and career of uncompromising fashion designer Alexander McQueen. Through a combination of archive footage and interviews with family, friends and colleagues, including Kate Moss, Jodie Kidd and Tom Ford, the film charts McQueen's story, taking in his childhood in the East End, his start as a tailor, launching and overseeing his eponymous line and his untimely death

programme.title: The Boy in the Striped Pyjamas (2008)
programme.desc: Bruno, a boy in Nazi Germany, moves to the country when his father is placed in charge of a concentration camp. While exploring the woods close to the facility's boundary, he spots a Jewish youngster being held there, and the two become friends - but Bruno remains innocently oblivious to the camp's true purpose. Drama, starring David Thewlis, Asa Butterfield and Jack Scanlon

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: Earnest 'Earn' Marks is broke, out of luck and living with his ex-girlfriend and their daughter, so when he learns that his cousin Alfred is on the verge of rap success under the name of Paper Boi, he sees an opportunity for both of them. First episode of the comedy drama, starring Donald Glover

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: The cousins are arrested after their shooting incident and while Paper Boi posts bail quickly, Earn is left at the station waiting to be processed along with everyone else who was arrested overnight. Meanwhile, the rapper is released to find that the incident has boosted his popularity

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: Earn spends all his money to get Paper Boi's song played on the radio, leaving him with no cash for his date with Van. Meanwhile, Alfred and Darius get deeper into the drugs trade

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter I, continuing her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Charlotte Smith are with four of the UK's leading shepherds and their sheepdogs for this year's Countryfile One Man and His Dog competition, which takes place on a tough course designed to test man and dog in the grounds of historic Hardwick Hall in Derbyshire. The coronavirus means things have to look a little different this year, with just one competitor from each home nation vying for glory. But this socially-distanced shepherding showdown is as testing and competitive as ever. The premier league of shepherding talent from England, Wales, Ireland and Scotland must battle it out over two rounds - the 'singles', where they run one dog, and the notoriously difficult 'brace', where they must work two dogs at the same time. Whoever gets the highest combined score will secure the pride of their nation and the coveted title

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Adam Frost celebrates some of the glorious gardens the programme has visited over the last few years and gives plenty of seasonal hints and tips for viewers to be doing in our own gardens. Frances Tophill visits a lochside retreat, Joe Swift finds lots of take home tips from a city garden, Carol Klein is inspired by plant combinations in West Yorkshire and Advolly Richmond shows how a historical landscape was designed for fun and frolic. Plus, the couple who let nature be their gardening guide and a gardener who makes containers her focus of attention

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: In a seasonal special, the Landward team celebrates one of the busiest and most exciting times in the countryside calendar - harvest. Dougie Vipond finds out about a new product making use of an ancient crop, Anne Lundon meets the farming family whose members have cut out the middle man to sell their product direct to brewers and Arlene Stuart looks at how the pandemic has affected the market for farmers' crops. Plus, Euan Mcilwraith meets the folk keeping the old ways alive with a vintage harvest and there's a look at a new hydrogen-powered tractor

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Kirsty Wilson takes a look at one of the oldest and rarest plants known to man, the Wollemi pine, sometimes called the fossil tree. George Anderson shows how to naturalise new bulbs in grass, and Carole Baxter, using techniques borrowed from her father, reviews her tomatoes and sees if her dad has it right

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Mary Berry's Simple Comforts
programme.desc: Mary Berry explores the world of comfort food, demonstrating how to make heartwarming, fuss-free dishes that can warm the cockles and lift the spirits. In the third episode, Mary visits Ireland, where wholesome comfort food is at the heart of their cuisine. Here, she makes a recipes inspired by the Emerald Isle, including a salmon and fennel one-pot wonder, and slow roast pork with crackling and buttery Colcannon mash

programme.title: Live Cycling
programme.desc: Jill Douglas presents coverage of the annual event, which is held this year in Imola, Italy. Denmark's Mads Pedersen was a surprise winner last year, winning the sprint in a three-man breakaway, with Italian Matteo Trentin and Stefan Kung of Switzerland taking silver and bronze respectively. With commentary from Simon Brotherton and Chris Boardman

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antiques experts Charles Hanson and Caroline Hawley head to the National Maritime Museum in Falmouth, Cornwall, where they unearth items of interest including a model yacht and a Newlyn copper tray. Meanwhile, presenter Paul Martin examines the work of controversial local artist Henry Scott Tuke

programme.title: North By Northwest (1959)
programme.desc: A suave advertising executive is mistaken for an American intelligence agent by a foreign spy and finds himself drawn into a world of espionage and adventure, leading to a high-tension cross-country chase made more perilous when the FBI intervenes. Alfred Hitchcock's thriller, starring Cary Grant, James Mason, Eva Marie Saint, Leo G Carroll and Martin Landau

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Dig
programme.desc: Yasmin Khan and Professor Alice Roberts go in search of the lost remains of some of the burial ground's more famous residents. These include Matthew Flinders, an explorer who expanded the British empire's domains to the then-uncharted coast of Southern Australia, and Bill Richmond, a champion boxer who was born in slavery in Colonial America and fought in the American War of Independence

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer fish for chub on the river Wye in Herefordshire, Bob making an unexpected arrival by rowing boat. They are joined on today's trip by Dr Anand Patel from the Centre for Men's Health for a discussion of medical matters, and spend the night in a converted dovecote in the idyllic grounds of a 700-year-old manor house

programme.title: Harry Hill's World of TV
programme.desc: Harry Hill focuses on the history of home improvement shows. From Do It Yourself with Barry Bucknell to Grand Designs, Harry reveals the tricks of the trade when making these shows

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: The film-maker looks back on his documentaries focused on families, including his trilogy of documentaries centred around the Westboro Baptist Church. He examines how the death of patriarch Fred Phelps has left members of the church divided. He also reflects on how physical and mental health or forces beyond control can threaten to derail family lives

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: Sam and the girls are invited to Marion and Caroline's housewarming, where Phil makes a scene. Troubled with ongoing pain, Sam visits a cannabis shop, and Frankie has some news

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: Sam and her single friends celebrate Father's Day with a party to commiserate with each other over their failed relationships. Phil has a very special present for Frankie

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Matt Parkman and Ted Sprague arrive in Texas to learn more about Mr Bennet, and take the entire family hostage - resulting in an explosive showdown. Claire discovers the truth about her adoptive father and powers. Hayden Panettiere and Jack Coleman star

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Nathan finally meets the mysterious and powerful Linderman. Suresh and Sylar make progress with the list and more horrors surface in Isaac's paintings. Meanwhile, Claire goes on the run and new hero Candice Wilmer appears. Fantasy drama, guest starring Malcolm McDowell

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: Paper Boi deals with his online presence after meeting a man named Zan the night before who then proceeds to troll him on social media. When Alfred later confronts Zan, he discovers that he is a pizza delivery boy and has a foul-mouthed child accomplice. Meanwhile, Earn, struggling with money troubles, tries to make a little extra cash with Darius, which results in trading a samurai sword for a male Cane Corso dog

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: A rival rapper steals the spotlight from Paper Boi at a charity basketball event, and Earn spots a chance to raise his professional profile when he is mistaken for someone else. Darius uses his free time to visit a shooting range

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: Van is irritated by her old friend Jayde, who is leading a much more successful life than she does. However, the pair find a way to put aside their differences while smoking weed - until Van remembers she is due a drug test at work

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Sue Perkins: Along the US-Mexico Border
programme.desc: Part one of two. The comedian travels along one of the world's most contentious borders, visiting places in America and Mexico to find out what life is like on both sides. Sue starts her journey at the western end of the border in the Mexican city of Tijuana, where she helps Honduran volunteers building a hostel for fellow refugees fleeing violence in their country

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Steve Fletcher experiences a blast from his past when he takes on restoring a vintage shop till, just like the ones he remembers from his childhood. Janine Savage from Dorset hopes wood aficionado Will Kirk can transform a very special dolls house custom made for her by her grandfather, while the Rev Richard Hayes arrives with a battered leather box containing a compact communion set, hoping its years of use can be made much less evident tghanks to the skills of Suzie Fletcher

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Couple Ian and Ruth run a 150-acre sheep farm in Rhayader, Powys, but don't have their own garden. Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich find themselves called upon to create a Beatrix Potter-inspired one in an empty field on a budget of £2,500

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: The presenters look to the future. George Anderson plants up a large container with what he calls a 'bulb lasagne', using different kinds of bulbs to produce layers of colour in the spring. Carole Baxter shows different methods of propagating new plants for next year, Chris Beardshaw plants a new border for year-round colour and Kirsty Wilson reveals why green roofs are the next big thing

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer head to Norfolk, where they attempt to ensnare a large pike - a fish that has eluded them on their trips so far. As they arrive and get their gear together, Bob introduces Paul to the surprisingly varied contents of his car boot, much to Paul's amusement. They gather their equipment and find a decent spot that should give them plenty of chances to catch and make camp for the day. They end their trip with a visit to a local pub, where Bob reveals a hidden trick

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by Alicia McCarthy

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Decimate
programme.desc: Quiz show, hosted by Shane Richie. A team of three contestants faces a golden wall of cash worth £20,000 and its members try to keep it intact as much of it as possible by correctly answering a series of questions

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin is in Clevedon, where artist John Webster and globetrotting retiree Caroline Crutchley learn the tricks of the antiques-buying trade to see who can make the most profit

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Coverage of Liberal Democrat leader Ed Davey's speech at the party's conference

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode continues his alphabetical tour through the BBC's culinary archive, this time selecting recipes and ingredients linked by the letter B. The programme includes clips of Levi Roots preparing beef pepperpot stew, and Nigel Slater cooking a spiced bread and butter pudding

programme.title: Cities: Nature's New Wild
programme.desc: Footage of animals that have made their homes in urban environments around the world. Featuring huge colonies of bats in Adelaide, smooth-coated otters at home in Singapore, and reticulated pythons living on the streets of Bangkok. Narrated by Daniel Kaluuya

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Will Axon and Christina Trevanion assess items at Ragley Hall in Warwickshire, including some intriguing decorative pictures, while Paul Martin looks at the man who saved the hall

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Chris Hollins, Gregg Wallace, Holly Walsh and Charlene White test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Inner-city farm manager Tom showcases his sheep at the Lambeth Country Show, while beekeeper Paul strives to sell his produce to support local charities close to his heart

programme.title: The Yorkshire Dales
programme.desc: Adventurer Paul Rose heads for Swaledale, joining farmers who look after rare upland hay meadows and enters the cake-making contest at Muker Show using a pressure cooker he has taken on a climb of Mount Everest. Paul also goes underground to try and find rare industrial artefacts left behind by lead miners in the 19th century and meets All Creatures Great and Small star Peter Davison

programme.title: Tom Kerridge: Lose Weight for Good
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, the chef takes the group of dieters on a river cruise to celebrate their weight loss over the past 12 weeks. Tom prepares a selection of sharing dishes for the special occasion, including couscous, griddled halloumi and Mediterranean vegetables, pulled pork tacos with salad, and falafels with a tzatziki dip

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts another first-round match as family team the Bridges take on murder mystery fans the Whodunnits. The sides must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues consisting of Printing, Compass, Papermaking, and Gunpowder

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The academic quiz's first-round matches continue, with four students from the University of Warwick pitting their wits against a team from Wolfson College, Oxford in a bid to reach the second stage of the competition. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: The Shipman Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: Chris Wilson re-examines the case of Harold Shipman, exploring how attitudes to the elderly enabled the GP to get away with the murder of hundreds of patients. In the first instalment, he reveals how the doctor's crimes were first discovered and how he was finally apprehended and charged with the murder of 15 of his elderly patients.

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and regular panellist Hugh Dennis look back on another eventful week in news and politics, with the help of fellow comedians Ed Byrne, Rhys James, Nish Kumar, Tiff Stevenson and Tom Allen. As usual, the contestants engage in various rounds of topical stand-up and improvisation as they try to get one up on each other

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti head to the Andean Cloud Forest of Ecuador to work at Mashpi Lodge, a $10 million modernist hotel featuring an extraordinary gondola cable car that ‘flies' guests one mile through the jungle canopy at a dizzying height

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events

programme.title: T20 Cricket
programme.desc: Action from the fourth match in the five-game series, held at Incora County Ground in Derby

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: Paper Boi becomes the hip-hop world's poster-child for toxic masculinity and transphobia after he makes some offensive tweets about Caitlyn Jenner, and ends up on a talk show - but the presenters are mistaken if they think they can get him to apologise

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: Paper Boi secures a gig playing a club, but neither he nor Earn are keen on the prospect, especially when they fall foul of a ruthless promoter unwilling to pay his dues and end up hosting the second most popular party in the room

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: Earn accompanies Van to an upmarket Juneteenth soiree, pretending to be a happily married couple with Ivy-League educations to try to help Van secure a networking opportunity with one of the many successful Atlanta power-players in attendance

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: The film-maker reflects on 25 years of his documentaries, featuring brand new conversations between Louis and some of his most memorable contributors

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Charlotte Smith are with four of the UK's leading shepherds and their sheepdogs for this year's Countryfile One Man and His Dog competition, which takes place on a tough course designed to test man and dog in the grounds of historic Hardwick Hall in Derbyshire. The coronavirus means things have to look a little different this year, with just one competitor from each home nation vying for glory. But this socially-distanced shepherding showdown is as testing and competitive as ever. The premier league of shepherding talent from England, Wales, Ireland and Scotland must battle it out over two rounds - the 'singles', where they run one dog, and the notoriously difficult 'brace', where they must work two dogs at the same time. Whoever gets the highest combined score will secure the pride of their nation and the coveted title

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Filmed in the autumn of 2019, cameras follow a new cast of characters from the London Ambulance Service as they care for the nine million people of the city. In the first episode, ambulance crew members Kate and Femi are dispatched to a young man who is struggling to breathe during a severe anxiety attack. Meanwhile, just three hours into the morning shift, control deal with the third assault of the day, having already responded to four stabbings

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein revisit the savage murder of a pub landlady in a sleepy Oxfordshire hamlet in 1922, which led to the unlikely conviction of a 15-year-old boy

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: The farmers gives their reflections on what each season means to them. In autumn, Argyll, Sybil and George gather their sheep from over six square miles of mountainside, while in the east of Scotland, Mel and Martin buy rams for their flock. In spring, Martin collects semen from his prize bull, John deals with an emergency caesarean, and Mel fights to save a poorly lamb's life. As summer arrives, the farmers can finally put their cattle in the fields, with Martin's having been indoors for eight months

programme.title: Pole to Pole
programme.desc: Michael Palin goes on safari in Kenya's Masai Mara. His journey continues through Tanzania to Ujiji - and on to the place where Victorian explorers Stanley and Livingstone became acquainted. Sailing down Lake Tanganyika in a dugout canoe, he anticipates a fairly straightforward route through Zambia, Zimbabwe and South Africa in the hope he will arrive in time to catch the annual boat trip from Cape Town to Antarctica

programme.title: Mystery Road
programme.desc: Rid of the human remains, Sandra's dig is disturbed by an intolerant tribal elder, while Jay presses Simon on his suspicions

programme.title: Mystery Road
programme.desc: Jay refuses to accept that the drugs case has been solved. Meanwhile, the forensics on the body in the bag add to Fran's distress

programme.title: The Beach: Isolation in Paradise
programme.desc: Film-maker Warwick Thornton gives up life in the fast lane - at least for a while - to go it alone on an isolated beach and see if he can transform and heal his life

programme.title: The Beach: Isolation in Paradise
programme.desc: Film-maker Warwick Thornton gives up life in the fast lane - at least for a while - to go it alone on an isolated beach and see if he can transform and heal his life

programme.title: Clive James: Postcard from Paris
programme.desc: In the 1960s as a young expatriate Australian writer, Clive James discovered Paris, which he felt was his spiritual home. In this programme he returns to the city and meets the writers, the models and the actresses who were part of a different world in his youth, and also encounters the unchanging arch-enemy of any visitor - the Paris taxi driver

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1981 Big Hits
programme.desc: The year 1981 embraced disco and ska, new wave punk, the burgeoning New Romantic scene and the rise of synthpop, with some prog quirkiness and good old rock'n'roll thrown in. Performances from big hitter soloists Phil Collins, Shakin' Stevens and Kim Wilde are featured alongside the exuberant chaos of groups like Tenpole Tudor, Adam and the Ants and the Teardrop Explodes. Plus, Odyssey, Clare Grogan, the Specials and the Human League

programme.title: Pole to Pole
programme.desc: Michael Palin goes on safari in Kenya's Masai Mara. His journey continues through Tanzania to Ujiji - and on to the place where Victorian explorers Stanley and Livingstone became acquainted. Sailing down Lake Tanganyika in a dugout canoe, he anticipates a fairly straightforward route through Zambia, Zimbabwe and South Africa in the hope he will arrive in time to catch the annual boat trip from Cape Town to Antarctica

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: The farmers gives their reflections on what each season means to them. In autumn, Argyll, Sybil and George gather their sheep from over six square miles of mountainside, while in the east of Scotland, Mel and Martin buy rams for their flock. In spring, Martin collects semen from his prize bull, John deals with an emergency caesarean, and Mel fights to save a poorly lamb's life. As summer arrives, the farmers can finally put their cattle in the fields, with Martin's having been indoors for eight months

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: The continued work of Australian naturalist Chris 'Brolga' Barnes, who has dedicated his life to raising kangaroos and is now opening his doors to new animals. However, it is not proving easy. The emu chicks are wreaking havoc, the camels are rapidly outgrowing their enclosure and the wombat is proving quite a handful. To help, Brolga is moving some of them to his 80-acre sanctuary - but after a clash with Roger the kangaroo, he has been left on crutches, and all his plans are on hold

programme.title: Woolly Mammoth: Secrets from the Ice
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores the natural history of the Ice Age mammals that once roamed across what is now Europe and Asia. Detailed information about their lives has been gleaned from carcasses preserved in the Siberian permafrost, and the anatomist meets scientists using genetic, chemical and molecular tests to reveal the adaptations that allowed mammoths to evolve from tropical animals to creatures capable of surviving extreme cold

programme.title: Black Classical Music: The Forgotten History
programme.desc: Lenny Henry and Suzy Klein celebrate black classical composers and musicians across the centuries whose stories and music have been forgotten

programme.title: Nicky and Wynton: The Making of a Concerto
programme.desc: The unique musical collaboration between American jazz legend Wynton Marsalis and Scottish violinist Nicola Benedetti, following the two musicians as they embark on a journey that culminates in the creation and performance of a violin concerto written by Marsalis especially for Benedetti. The film observes Wynton and Nicola during the process of composition, from the pair batting ideas and drafts back and forth across the Atlantic, to rehearsals in the UK and US, leading to the world premiere of the concerto with the London Symphony Orchestra at London's Barbican. Narrated by Eve Best

programme.title: Nana Mouskouri at the BBC
programme.desc: A collection of the singer's performances from the BBC archive, including her collaborations with Michel Legrand, Cliff Richard and Charles Aznavour, as well as A Force de Prier, the song with which she represented Luxembourg in the 1963 Eurovision Song Contest

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Peggy reveals Roxy's hiding-place to Ronnie, Phil takes on a special errand for Max, and Billy and Honey step up when their family needs them

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Peggy's secret from Ronnie is revealed, Roxy is having the time of her life, and Phil's errand does not go as planned

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ronnie struggles to get through to her sister, Archie and Peggy get reacquainted, and back in Walford, Phil gets more than he bargained for from Suzy

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Tensions run high as Ronnie and Archie open old wounds, Sean attempts to win over Roxy, and Peggy makes a big decision

programme.title: Earth's Great Rivers
programme.desc: Documentary following three of the world's largest rivers, beginning with the Amazon, uncovering some of the latest discoveries, including the 'Blue Lagoon' which is home to a newly discovered species of cichlid. The programme also explores some of the Amazon's flooded forests, home to an array of unusual animals, and cameras follow giant river otters on a fishing foray on the rocky terraces of the Cristalino river

programme.title: Timothy Spall: Somewhere at Sea
programme.desc: The actor and his wife Shane embark on a journey from Cardiff to Clydebank in their barge the Princess Matilda. The first leg of the voyage begins smoothly as they travel to Milford Haven - but a bungled departure leaves them marooned en route to Fishguard. Shane also gets to make an emotional return to Aberystwyth, and the couple receive a pleasant surprise when they visit the coastal village of Porthdinllaen

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene depicting the beauty of a shimmering pool gently cradled by a soft, beautiful, bright forest

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Nicky Philipps, an artist who has previously painted portraits of the royal family, seeks help in determining whether a mysterious painting housed at Picton Castle in Pembrokeshire is a genuine work by celebrated French Impressionist Pierre Auguste Renoir. Picton Castle is the ancestral home of Nicky's family, and her aunt used to tell an intriguing tale that the picture in question came from Claude Monet's house in Giverny, and was originally a gift given to the artist by Renoir himself. Fiona Bruce heads to Monet's home to determine whether this rumour is true, and investigates contrasting academic opinions on whether the painting is genuine. Meanwhile, Philip Mould travels to the Parisian suburb of Argenteuil and to Berlin to make use of cutting-edge technology to determine whether the pigments in Nicky's picture match up with those listed as being used by Renoir

programme.title: The Secret History of Writing
programme.desc: Lydia Wilson examines the changing technology of writing through the centuries, looking at how it has shaped the course of history and been a factor in the rise and fall of empires

programme.title: Charles I: Killing a King
programme.desc: Documentary about the execution of Charles I, beginning by following the events of Christmas 1648. The king was visited at Windsor by the Earl of Denbigh, seeking to broker terms between the imprisoned monarch and an increasingly hardline parliament. When his visit was rejected, new legislation is drawn up to try the king for treason

programme.title: Handmade: By Royal Appointment
programme.desc: Documentary celebrating handcrafted objects made under the royal warrant, beginning by following the creation of a Wedgwood panther vase by artisan potters. The programme also takes a look at how this quintessentially British company is reinventing itself for the 21st century under new, foreign ownership

programme.title: Sold! Inside the World's Biggest Auction House
programme.desc: The second of a two-part documentary going behind the scenes at Christie's, where Global President Jussi Pylkkanen and his team continue with their big push into Asia. As the company prepares for its sale to celebrate its 250th anniversary, an umbrella stand turns out to be worth millions and a Rubens appears that has not been seen in public for 150 years

programme.desc: In this episode Dr. Maggie reveals the gamechangers of space – missions and spacecraft from the Space Shuttle to the Hubble Space Telescope that have transformed our lives on earth and beyond. She flew in the space shuttle three times, she delivered Hubble into orbit and was the first American woman to walk in space! Plus, Dr Maggie counts down the Top 5 Most Expensive Missions and all your space questions are answered in Ask Maggie. Buckle up, it’s time to go Out Of This World!

programme.title: Horrible Histories
programme.desc: Marking the eightieth anniversary of the outbreak of World War II, Horrible Histories looks at how the war affected those left back home in the UK, on the home front. We start with the initial panic following the declaration of war and the measures taken at home to prepare for an imminent Nazi invasion, which didn't actually turn out to be that imminent at all. However, the Nazis are busy planning their invasion - Operation Sea Lion is being carefully orchestrated to avoid damaging places in the UK that they have previously been to on holiday. We see how the blackout during the Blitz affected everyday life, and also how King George and Queen Elizabeth refused to leave Buckingham Palace while London was being bombed, despite being advised to head to Canada by their staff. We meet the 'ATA Girls' - the heroic women of the Air Transport Auxiliary led by Pauline Gower.
