programme.desc: Double trouble as Four splits and a pair of tricky twins turn up - the Terrible Twos, who decide it's time to tickle their friends to pieces

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: The bunny finds a replacement blanket when his needs to go in the wash

programme.title: Messy Goes to OKIDO
programme.desc: Science-educational series following a curious, adventure-seeking and unruly monster as he finds the answers to everything a pre-school child would want to know

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friend arrange a disco party, and Mr Tumble holds one of his own.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies say 'Eh-oh' to one another in turn and then to the flowers, while the children in Tummy Tales make a friendship circle out of handprints

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: Gurdy is having lots of little ideas, who run all over the tree. There's no room for the Olobobs! So they create Pie Piper, who enchants the ideas and gives them a yummy pie

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: It's a hot sunny day, and Pablo is running around when his head starts to hurt

programme.title: What's the Big Idea?
programme.desc: Hugo tries to find out what art is by looking at different kinds of paintings, sculptures and photos

programme.title: Time for School
programme.desc: Observational documentary on the lives of reception children at two contrasting primary schools - one rural, and one urban

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Captain Sinker enters a 'selfie competition' in the hope of winning the best holiday ever, and three youngsters play Shivering Timbers, Wacky Whirlpool and Shipwreck Rummage

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: A big package arrives for Duggee while he is dusting - an amazing new vacuum cleaner

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: The mischievous rabbit is asked to mind Ginger and Pickles' store, and he quickly discovers running a shop is no easy feat, especially with customers like Sammy Whiskers

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Love Monster is carefully putting the finishing touches to a set of cards that he is going to send out for Red Envelope Day. But after accidentally knocking into Delivery Duckling, the cards get mixed up, and he sends out Elder Kitten's envelope without his card in it! Love Monster must chase down Delivery Duckling and stop her from making the delivery.

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie Moate explores the house, using a special camera and animation to see how a key works inside a lock and shows how the hidden barrel and pins inside the lock work

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: Princess Purl has one more task to complete to stay a Princess. She must dance with the very rare Zippopopomus before the castle bell rings three times. If she fails, she stops being a princess

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Lars zooms to the rescue when Glitch and Tala go off the rails in search of a baby dragon.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle ends up in Evie's class, when he must be still so the headteacher doesn't see him.

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo has a sleepover at Gran Gran's, but bedtime comes while it's still light outside.

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: After a night of strong rain, the Shallots set sail on a seafaring adventure

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Tiny and Small are in the music boat when they find a sticky green pod on a Sky Moo's snout. Animated adventure, narrated by Michael Palin

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Igglepiggle is enjoying dancing in the garden. He leaves his blanket in a safe place, but when he returns it has gone. The blanket has landed on Makka Pakka and seems to be running about by itself

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Helen George reads a bedtime Story about a cat that has a special chair

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Sally Nugent present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Host Matt Tebbutt is joined by chef Freddy Bird, along with a special guest. Plus, clips of Rick Stein, Keith Floyd, Nadiya Hussain and Sam Evans

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: In the third episode, Nadiya Hussain shares some of her favourite savoury dishes, including a chicken and pink peppercorn pie, and simple, baked teriyaki noodles which are the perfect fuss-free midweek meal. Nadiya also demonstrates how to make a tear and share bread loaf with all the flavours of a pizza

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's Premier League fixtures, including Brighton & Hove Albion v Manchester United and Sheffield Untied v Leeds United

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Thomas Forrester and Kate Bliss help two teams scour an antiques fair in Newark, Nottinghamshire, and Anita Manning learns about the rescue and restoration of a historic barge

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is in rural Devon with an ambitious couple looking for a large country home and as much acreage as their £1.25million budget will afford them

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore salvages an old desk, a pink armchair, a rusty old bucket and an unwanted painting, handing them out for renovation before giving any profits to the owners

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway treks from Harlech to Barmouth, stripping down to basics as he crosses a nudist beach in Gwynedd. On this walk along one of Wales' most popular coastlines, Derek also rolls a cheese down the world's steepest hill and learns about the 'Devil in the Shawl' when he visits an old village chapel. Along his journey, he nearly gets caught out on a tidal causeway and meets a team of women who give up their free time to keep the beaches clean and safe for wildlife

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special comedians edition of the general knowledge quiz, featuring Ed Byrne, Jessie Cave, Dane Baptiste, Shazia Mirza, Angela Barnes, Rich Hall, Patrick Monahan and Junior Simpson

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the return of the music-based quiz show, in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs for the winners. Across four rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits across various decades. In the first episode, mother and daughter Julie and Lauren from Edinburgh, partners Ben and Adam from Staffordshire, and married couple Jess and John from Liverpool take part

programme.title: Little Mix The Search
programme.desc: The girl group search for the next global singing sensation, with the winning band joining them on their next UK tour. In the first edition, Perrie, Jesy, Leigh-Anne and Jade put together a boyband. The talented hopefuls are auditioned individually, before being put through their paces alongside their competitors. Only the most impressive will make it through to the final line up to battle it out for the prize in the performance shows

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: A selection of the nation's favourite moments from themed weeks on Strictly. This time, Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman, the judges and the show's professional dancers look back at some of the most memorable Strictly performances at Blackpool's famous ballroom, from the super-sized dances to the high-octane group numbers

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Hospital drama with the staff of Holby's accident and emergency department as they deal with more sick and injured patients. Marty reconnects with his homophobic father, and Rash is forced to intervene as disaster strikes the ED and the team are thrown into chaos. Starring Shaheen Jafargholi and Neet Mohan

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures, including Brighton & Hove Albion v Manchester United at Amex Stadium and West Bromwich Albion v Chelsea at The Hawthorns. United will have been looking for a repeat of their comfortable 3-0 victory here last term, while Chelsea won 4-0 the last time they visited the Baggies in November 2017

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman, Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell discuss the latest talking points from the NFL and look ahead to the weekend's action

programme.title: Man Up (2015)
programme.desc: While traveling across London to attend a party to celebrate her parents' 10th wedding anniversary, a thirtysomething single woman is mistaken for a recently divorced stranger's blind date, so decides to spend a chaotic evening with him. However, she has to face the consequences when he realises she is not his intended blind date. Romantic comedy, starring Lake Bell and Simon Pegg

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Ben Thompson and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures, including Brighton & Hove Albion v Manchester United at Amex Stadium and West Bromwich Albion v Chelsea at The Hawthorns. United will have been looking for a repeat of their comfortable 3-0 victory here last term, while Chelsea won 4-0 the last time they visited the Baggies in November 2017

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: James Williams presents the latest political news and debate, and reports from around Wales

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Rebecca Ellis wants a new start with partner Kylie and children in Australia, so they set out to enjoy a trial week in Brisbane. Nicki Chapman follows their fortunes

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Raj Bisram help two teams scour an antiques fair in Harrogate, North Yorkshire, while Natasha Raskin Sharp finds out what got David Harper hooked on collecting

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Claire McCollum explores the Victorian model village of Saltaire in West Yorkshire, which was built by a Christian mill owner to transform the lives of his workers

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Ricky Wilson presents an appeal on behalf of the Teenage Cancer Trust, a national charity offering care and support services to young people living with cancer

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades is at Walsall recycling centre on a mission to save three unwanted items, rescuing a well-used table, a retro chair and rusty gate rods from the skip

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: On a route from Pwll to Kidwelly, Derek Brockway turns Pied Piper as he launches a walk of 800 children marking the anniversary of 'People Power' that saved a stretch of the Loughor Estuary for families to enjoy. On this Carmarthenshire coastal walk, the weatherman also discovers a former munitions factory that employed 6,000 people, has a spin in a racing car and learns the story Wales' feisty warrior princess Gwenllian

programme.title: A Special School
programme.desc: Rebecca Jones introduces her class of eight autistic boys, each with their own individual needs, including Ethan - who likes to answer the school's telephone. Fifteen-year-old Luke was born with schizencephaly - a hole in his brain - which has left him with long-term physical health problems. Ysgol y Deri is his lifeline and with voice-generating software, teachers are helping him to express his emotions

programme.title: Hidden Wales with Will Millard
programme.desc: Welsh slate once roofed the buildings of the British Empire and by the late 1800s a third of all roofing slates on the planet came from north Wales. The industry produced communities, culture and an identity that are still alive today - but much of the history has been forgotten or vanished. Will travels to the region to track down some of the remains and relics from this vibrant period of industry by exploring deep inside the mountains of Snowdonia in some of biggest slate mines in the world

programme.title: Coal House
programme.desc: The women begin to feel the strain of life in the 1920s with mouths to feed and cleaning routines to stick to, but there is some light relief as the Cartwrights, Phillips and Griffiths families enjoy a rare night out, and celebrate a very special birthday.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Joe Crowley are in the Lake District to celebrate the 250th anniversary of William Wordsworth's birth, and also mark 50 years of Langdale and Ambleside Mountain Rescue. Plus, Adam Henson find out where he will get the best prices for his rams - online or at a traditional livestock market?

programme.title: Little Mix The Search
programme.desc: The group put together a mixed band of boys and girls, with singers auditioned individually and then tested alongside their competitors before the final line-up is decided, after a nerve-shredding intimate performance in front of Little Mix and a crowd of their fans

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce shares unseen items from recent roadshows, from a set of designer jewellery to an ancient strong-box and some engraved glasses which might not be all that they seem. Also among the eclectic finds are brooches handed down from the royal family, a spy camera, a prop from a St Trinian's film and a hand-cut paper Valentine designed to win a hand in marriage

programme.title: Us
programme.desc: Despite a rocky start in Paris, the Petersen family's European tour continues in Amsterdam where it seems that nothing can prevent the family from getting into the holiday spirit, not even a sleazy hotel that might actually be a brothel. But an explosive fight at breakfast looks like it might be the end of the line for their trip

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including Sheffield United v Leeds United at Bramall Lane and Manchester City v Leicester City at Etihad Stadium. Leeds are making a long-awaited return to top-flight football and will be looking to emulate their local rivals, who finished in ninth place last term. The Citizens prevailed 3-1 when they met Leicester here last season, but their opponents pushed them for second place for much of the campaign before fading to fifth after the restart. Plus, Tottenham Hotspur v Newcastle United, West Ham United v Wolverhampton Wanderers and a round-up of yesterday's goals

programme.title: Squad Goals
programme.desc: The January transfer window sees two new arrivals at West Ham United, but a mysterious virus begins to take its grip across the squad when the team return after the Christmas break

programme.title: The Water Diviner (2014)
programme.desc: An Australian farmer's life is shattered during the First World War, when he receives news that his three sons have been declared missing in action during the battle of Gallipoli. In the aftermath of the conflict, he travels to Turkey in the hope of discovering the truth about their fate. Period drama, directed by and starring Russell Crowe, with Olga Kurylenko

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: The return of the documentary using bodycam and CCTV footage to tell the stories of emergency service workers assaulted in the line of duty. Police officers are doused in petrol and threatened with being set alight when a moped chase ends in chaos

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin follow the development progress of properties in Brompton in Kent, Workington in Cumbria and Great Barr in the West Midlands

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: The England Illegal Money Lending Team target a suspect loan shark and raid his home, and a convicted loan shark has a caravan seized in Wales

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: Investigators uncover a senior manager gambling with an NHS credit card, and tackle the cowards targeting vulnerable elderly women for their life savings

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Richard Madley and Stephanie Connell help two teams search for items in Brackley, Northamptonshire, while presenter Eric Knowles discovers a marvel of Victorian timekeeping

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Indian Doctor
programme.desc: High-flying Delhi graduate Dr Prem Sharma and his wife Kamini move to the UK after being wooed by dreams of riches and glamour, only to find themselves residing in a small Welsh mining village. While Prem strikes up new friendships and settles into life in the Valleys, Kamini wants them to leave as soon as possible. Comedy drama set in the 1960s, starring Sanjeev Bhaskar and Ayesha Dharker

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers celebrate quality produce that needs to be cooked with the respect it deserves, as they continue their culinary journey through time. The duo argue that, while convenience food serves a purpose, only the best ingredients and perfect cooking can produce something truly delicious

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a couple of keen horticulturalists who have £450,000 to find a home in the Yorkshire Dales, and also visits RHS Garden Harlow Carr to meet Alan Titchmarsh

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers have a £5,500 budget to create a dramatic Balinese garden full of greenery and tropical plants for newlyweds Peter and Samantha in Cheltenham

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades oversees proceedings as the show's experts restore an 80-year-old banjo, a pair of 1930s rollerskates and a 19th-century wooden salad bowl

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat. Presented by Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The programme hears from whistleblowers working inside the Government's new coronavirus tracking system. They are so concerned about NHS Test and Trace that they are speaking out to reveal chaos, technical problems, confusion, wasted resources and a system that does not appear to them to be working. There are also interviews with members of local public health teams, who say they have largely been ignored by the Government in favour of the private companies hired to run the new centralised tracking system

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian hosts a business meeting at home, determined to impress a council colleague with his hospitality skills - only for Max to storm in making accusations and threats. Isaac is reeling after his meeting with the school about the drugs, leading to an altercation with Phil, and Mick is disturbed to find Frankie taking photos of Ollie. Kheerat tries to explain himself to Karen, and Rainie and Stuart say their farewells on the night before their wedding

programme.title: A Special School
programme.desc: Nurse Leanne responds to a medical emergency when a pupil has a seizure and is having difficulty breathing, while hairdresser Claire volunteers her time in the school's salon to work with pupils who find it extremely difficult and sometimes painful to have their hair

programme.title: Freddie Flintoff: Living with Bulimia
programme.desc: The former England cricketer faces up to his eating disorder of 20 years as he challenges the stigma around the condition in men. He explores how bulimia has played a part in the course of his life, discusses his own experience in detail and meets specialists and young men with eating disorders across the UK

programme.title: Stand Up for Live Comedy
programme.desc: Showcase for emerging talent, filmed in six different cities across the UK in outdoor locations with socially distanced audiences. The first edition is from Bristol, where local comedian Jayde Adams plays host to Mo Omar, Lauren Pattison and Tom Lucy

programme.title: Yung Filly's Celeb Lock-In
programme.desc: The host is joined by Wayne and Frankie Bridge on the game show, with the couple helping four Geordie households competing to win the grand prize

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Actor, writer and TV presenter Richard Ayoade takes over the reins for an extended 2019 edition of the satirical quiz, with Pointless co-host Richard Osman and comedian Kiri Pritchard-McLean joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the stories making the headlines

programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich are called in by a young family in Fishponds, Bristol, to create a garden to unwind in after work. Zoe and Jonny are dedicated charity workers who want and need a sanctuary spot to relax away from their emotionally challenging work. They also want the designers to find a way of including a place for Zoe to grow her wedding bouquet for their upcoming marriage, somewhere for new baby, Ottilie, to play and even an outdoor spot for Zoe to practise alfresco yoga

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore turns to design duo Josh and Oli to repurpose an old mower, while upholsterer Leigh-Anne Treadwell goes out on a limb with her redesign of a pair of teak chairs. Sarah's challenge is to find new homes for three Persian rugs. Narrated by Arthur Smith

programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a pair of teachers with a £600,000 budget who want to upsize and find the ideal country home for them and their four young children. Away from property, Jonnie turns the heat up on his Lancastrian tour when he visits one of the last working forges in the county

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Footage of wildlife that inhabit remote islands, revealing the advantages and disadvantages of the creatures' habitat. Three-toed sloths in the Caribbean and nesting albatross enjoy a peaceful existence untroubled by predators, but other animals are not so fortunate. Marine iguanas in the Galapagos Islands are preyed on by racer snakes from birth, while penguins on the Antarctic island of Zavodovski risk being battered against the rocks by fierce waves

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Steven Thompson presents extended highlights from Sunday's Scottish Premiership fixtures - Motherwell v Rangers and Celtic v Hibernian. Plus, a look back at Saturday's games

programme.title: Squad Goals
programme.desc: The January transfer window sees two new arrivals at West Ham United, but a mysterious virus begins to take its grip across the squad when the team return after the Christmas break

programme.title: The Water Diviner (2014)
programme.desc: An Australian farmer's life is shattered during the First World War, when he receives news that his three sons have been declared missing in action during the battle of Gallipoli. In the aftermath of the conflict, he travels to Turkey in the hope of discovering the truth about their fate. Period drama, directed by and starring Russell Crowe, with Olga Kurylenko

programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: The return of the documentary using bodycam and CCTV footage to tell the stories of emergency service workers assaulted in the line of duty. Police officers are doused in petrol and threatened with being set alight when a moped chase ends in chaos

programme.title: The Indian Doctor
programme.desc: High-flying Delhi graduate Dr Prem Sharma and his wife Kamini move to the UK after being wooed by dreams of riches and glamour, only to find themselves residing in a small Welsh mining village. While Prem strikes up new friendships and settles into life in the Valleys, Kamini wants them to leave as soon as possible. Comedy drama set in the 1960s, starring Sanjeev Bhaskar and Ayesha Dharker

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers celebrate quality produce that needs to be cooked with the respect it deserves, as they continue their culinary journey through time. programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a couple of keen horticulturalists who have £450,000 to find a home in the Yorkshire Dales, and also visits RHS Garden Harlow Carr to meet Alan Titchmarsh

programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers have a £5,500 budget to create a dramatic Balinese garden full of greenery and tropical plants for newlyweds Peter and Samantha in Cheltenham

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades oversees proceedings as the show's experts restore an 80-year-old banjo, a pair of 1930s rollerskates and a 19th-century wooden salad bowl

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat. Presented by Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The programme hears from whistleblowers working inside the Government's new coronavirus tracking system. They are so concerned about NHS Test and Trace that they are speaking out to reveal chaos, technical problems, confusion, wasted resources and a system that does not appear to them to be working. There are also interviews with members of local public health teams, who say they have largely been ignored by the Government in favour of the private companies hired to run the new centralised tracking system

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian hosts a business meeting at home, determined to impress a council colleague with his hospitality skills - only for Max to storm in making accusations and threats. Isaac is reeling after his meeting with the school about the drugs, leading to an altercation with Phil, and Mick is disturbed to find Frankie taking photos of Ollie. Kheerat tries to explain himself to Karen, and Rainie and Stuart say their farewells on the night before their wedding

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: After a heavy night partying, Alison and Mike wake to find their house in disarray and with the clock ticking until a potentially crucial business partner comes to view the venue, the couple attempt a mass clean up. The ghosts are all acting very strangely, and Alison struggles to piece together the events of the night before to figure out what happened to them

programme.title: Freddie Flintoff: Living with Bulimia
programme.desc: The former England cricketer faces up to his eating disorder of 20 years as he challenges the stigma around the condition in men. He explores how bulimia has played a part in the course of his life, discusses his own experience in detail and meets specialists and young men with eating disorders across the UK

programme.title: Stand Up for Live Comedy
programme.desc: Showcase for emerging talent, filmed in six different cities across the UK in outdoor locations with socially distanced audiences. The first edition is from Bristol, where local comedian Jayde Adams plays host to Mo Omar, Lauren Pattison and Tom Lucy

programme.title: Yung Filly's Celeb Lock-In
programme.desc: The host is joined by Wayne and Frankie Bridge on the game show, with the couple helping four Geordie households competing to win the grand prize

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Actor, writer and TV presenter Richard Ayoade takes over the reins for an extended 2019 edition of the satirical quiz, with Pointless co-host Richard Osman and comedian Kiri Pritchard-McLean joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the stories making the headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Sally Nugent present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Host Matt Tebbutt is joined by chef Freddy Bird, along with a special guest. Plus, clips of Rick Stein, Keith Floyd, Nadiya Hussain and Sam Evans

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: In the third episode, Nadiya Hussain shares some of her favourite savoury dishes, including a chicken and pink peppercorn pie, and simple, baked teriyaki noodles which are the perfect fuss-free midweek meal. Nadiya also demonstrates how to make a tear and share bread loaf with all the flavours of a pizza

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's Premier League fixtures, including Brighton & Hove Albion v Manchester United and Sheffield Untied v Leeds United

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Thomas Forrester and Kate Bliss help two teams scour an antiques fair in Newark, Nottinghamshire, and Anita Manning learns about the rescue and restoration of a historic barge

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is in rural Devon with an ambitious couple looking for a large country home and as much acreage as their £1.25million budget will afford them

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich are called in by a young family in Fishponds, Bristol, to create a garden to unwind in after work. Zoe and Jonny are dedicated charity workers who want and need a sanctuary spot to relax away from their emotionally challenging work. They also want the designers to find a way of including a place for Zoe to grow her wedding bouquet for their upcoming marriage, somewhere for new baby, Ottilie, to play and even an outdoor spot for Zoe to practise alfresco yoga

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore turns to design duo Josh and Oli to repurpose an old mower, while upholsterer Leigh-Anne Treadwell goes out on a limb with her redesign of a pair of teak chairs. Sarah's challenge is to find new homes for three Persian rugs. Narrated by Arthur Smith

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special comedians edition of the general knowledge quiz, featuring Ed Byrne, Jessie Cave, Dane Baptiste, Shazia Mirza, Angela Barnes, Rich Hall, Patrick Monahan and Junior Simpson

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the return of the music-based quiz show, in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs for the winners. Across four rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits across various decades. In the first episode, mother and daughter Julie and Lauren from Edinburgh, partners Ben and Adam from Staffordshire, and married couple Jess and John from Liverpool take part

programme.title: Little Mix The Search
programme.desc: The girl group search for the next global singing sensation, with the winning band joining them on their next UK tour. In the first edition, Perrie, Jesy, Leigh-Anne and Jade put together a boyband. The talented hopefuls are auditioned individually, before being put through their paces alongside their competitors. Only the most impressive will make it through to the final line up to battle it out for the prize in the performance shows

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: A selection of the nation's favourite moments from themed weeks on Strictly. This time, Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman, the judges and the show's professional dancers look back at some of the most memorable Strictly performances at Blackpool's famous ballroom, from the super-sized dances to the high-octane group numbers

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Hospital drama with the staff of Holby's accident and emergency department as they deal with more sick and injured patients. Marty reconnects with his homophobic father, and Rash is forced to intervene as disaster strikes the ED and the team are thrown into chaos. Starring Shaheen Jafargholi and Neet Mohan

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures, including Brighton & Hove Albion v Manchester United at Amex Stadium and West Bromwich Albion v Chelsea at The Hawthorns. United will have been looking for a repeat of their comfortable 3-0 victory here last term, while Chelsea won 4-0 the last time they visited the Baggies in November 2017

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman, Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell discuss the latest talking points from the NFL and look ahead to the weekend's action

programme.title: Man Up (2015)
programme.desc: While traveling across London to attend a party to celebrate her parents' 10th wedding anniversary, a thirtysomething single woman is mistaken for a recently divorced stranger's blind date, so decides to spend a chaotic evening with him. However, she has to face the consequences when he realises she is not his intended blind date. Romantic comedy, starring Lake Bell and Simon Pegg

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Ben Thompson and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures, including Brighton & Hove Albion v Manchester United at Amex Stadium and West Bromwich Albion v Chelsea at The Hawthorns. programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers looks at the political developments of the week and questions policy-makers on the key issues

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Rebecca Ellis wants a new start with partner Kylie and children in Australia, so they set out to enjoy a trial week in Brisbane. Nicki Chapman follows their fortunes

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Raj Bisram help two teams scour an antiques fair in Harrogate, North Yorkshire, while Natasha Raskin Sharp finds out what got David Harper hooked on collecting

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Claire McCollum explores the Victorian model village of Saltaire in West Yorkshire, which was built by a Christian mill owner to transform the lives of his workers

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley

programme.title: Community Life
programme.desc: This edition features the Woodland Trust, which is looking for new volunteers, while the Book Trust has been helping younger readers to discover the world of books

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Footage of wildlife that inhabit remote islands, revealing the advantages and disadvantages of the creatures' habitat. Three-toed sloths in the Caribbean and nesting albatross enjoy a peaceful existence untroubled by predators, but other animals are not so fortunate. Marine iguanas in the Galapagos Islands are preyed on by racer snakes from birth, while penguins on the Antarctic island of Zavodovski risk being battered against the rocks by fierce waves

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a pair of teachers with a £600,000 budget who want to upsize and find the ideal country home for them and their four young children. Away from property, Jonnie turns the heat up on his Lancastrian tour when he visits one of the last working forges in the county

programme.title: Proms in Your Garden
programme.desc: John Toal presents performances by Barry Douglas, Gavin James, Lisa McHugh, George Hutton and Snow Patrol, and revisits memorable Proms moments from the Ulster Orchestra

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Joe Crowley are in the Lake District to celebrate the 250th anniversary of William Wordsworth's birth, and also mark 50 years of Langdale and Ambleside Mountain Rescue. Plus, Adam Henson find out where he will get the best prices for his rams - online or at a traditional livestock market?

programme.title: Little Mix The Search
programme.desc: The group put together a mixed band of boys and girls, with singers auditioned individually and then tested alongside their competitors before the final line-up is decided, after a nerve-shredding intimate performance in front of Little Mix and a crowd of their fans

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce shares unseen items from recent roadshows, from a set of designer jewellery to an ancient strong-box and some engraved glasses which might not be all that they seem. Also among the eclectic finds are brooches handed down from the royal family, a spy camera, a prop from a St Trinian's film and a hand-cut paper Valentine designed to win a hand in marriage

programme.title: Us
programme.desc: Despite a rocky start in Paris, the Petersen family's European tour continues in Amsterdam where it seems that nothing can prevent the family from getting into the holiday spirit, not even a sleazy hotel that might actually be a brothel. But an explosive fight at breakfast looks like it might be the end of the line for their trip

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including Sheffield United v Leeds United at Bramall Lane and Manchester City v Leicester City at Etihad Stadium. Leeds are making a long-awaited return to top-flight football and will be looking to emulate their local rivals, who finished in ninth place last term. The Citizens prevailed 3-1 when they met Leicester here last season, but their opponents pushed them for second place for much of the campaign before fading to fifth after the restart. Plus, Tottenham Hotspur v Newcastle United, West Ham United v Wolverhampton Wanderers and a round-up of yesterday's goals

programme.desc: Dennis recalls his youth in Coalisland and rise on the professional snooker circuit. While Dennis often reached tournament finals, he always seemed to falter - leading the press to class him as an eternal runner-up. programme.desc: Scooby-Doo and the gang travel to Chambersburg, a battlefield near Gettysburg, to investigate the mysterious appearance of ghost zombie Civil War soldiers.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The teenage superheroes fight to save the world while getting up to mischievous antics

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined in the kitchen by guest Rob Rinder and chefs Kuba Winkowski and Galton Blackiston. Featuring recipes for sweet and sour chicken, seafood biryani, trout, pork stew, and whisky meringue, and a masterclass in making fudge

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott prepares dishes inspired by school dinners, including shepherd's pie with minted peas, mac and cheese with bacon and leeks, and jam and coconut sponge with custard. He is joined by guests Kimberly Wyatt, who whips up a cheese and bean tortilla, and Juliet Sear, who puts her own spin on a classic school dessert with her cornflake tart

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: The host and travelling companion Nick Nairn continue their Scottish adventure in Lossiemouth, home to the first RAF squadron and a fleet of typhoon fighter jets

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott enter the world of botany to search for the killer of a retired teacher found dead shortly after smuggling a rare orchid out of Borneo. The discovery of two more bodies makes the detectives realise the murderer will stop at nothing to get hold of the coveted plant, and it seems the key to the mystery may lie in a Latin notebook. John Nettles and John Hopkins star

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Family Fortunes
programme.desc: Gino D'Acampo takes over hosting duties on this revamped version of the popular game show, which sees two families trying to guess the answers to survey-based questions posed to 100 people. And for anyone who hasn't seen it, the family who score the most points go through to the final round, where they play for the a £30,000 jackpot

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity special of the classic game show, with Nicola Coughlan, Stephen Mangan and Toyah Willcox all trying to say what they see as they guess well-known phrases for the chance to win a £50,000 jackpot for their chosen charity

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents a special Emmerdale episode of the quiz show, as celebrity contestants Anthony Quinlan, Louisa Clein, James Moore and Dominic Brunt pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds for charity. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent
programme.desc: Ant and Dec host the fourth semi-final of this year's talent contest, making a return to screens following the auditions episodes broadcast in the spring. Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon, David Walliams and Ashley Banjo are on the judging desk, as the acts that impressed them the most return, including the golden buzzer acts - singer Fayth Ifil, comedian and singer Jon Courtney, choir Sign Along With Us, comedian Nabil Abdulrashid, and mother and daughter singing duo, Honey and Sammy. Eight acts perform in each semi-final, with two finalists selected by the judges and the votes of viewers

programme.title: Jonathan Ross' Comedy Club
programme.desc: Jonathan and co-host Mawaan Rizwan present another stand-up showcase featuring some of the best and funniest new comedians in the UK. This episode features sets from Lloyd Griffith, Joanne McNally and Kemah Bob, while Tom Allen will be trying out some new material

programme.title: Bad Neighbours (2014)
programme.desc: A couple living in suburbia and adjusting to new parenthood are determined to prove they can still be cool when a household of students moves in next door. However, when their raucous neighbours' wild parties disrupt their lives, they decide to fight back, leading to a vicious cycle of one-upmanship. Comedy, starring Seth Rogen, Zac Efron and Rose Byrne

programme.title: Britain's Brightest Family
programme.desc: Anne Hegerty hosts a quiz show in which 16 families take part in a knock out tournament as they attempt to win the holiday of a lifetime and the title of Britain's Brightest Family. In the second episode, the Collier family from Cambridgeshire and the Brolly family from Co Antrim, Northern Ireland, battle for a place in the quarter-finals

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Alan is joined by Ray Mears, who rustles up a campfire breakfast, and Fern Britton, who talks about her love for Cornwall. He also remembers a day he spent at the allotment with Mary Berry and welcomes some rare breed pigs to the farm. Plus, more from the organic halal farming family the Radwans

programme.title: All Around Britain
programme.desc: Ranvir Singh and Alex Beresford visit Neasden Temple in London as they present the best of ITV regional news programmes. Stories include the West Midlands doctor who performed a DJ set at the virtual V-Fest during lockdown and the family from Cambridge who have moved to the most northerly point of Northern Ireland - the remote Rathlin Island

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh makes over a garden in Newark for a double-amputee veteran Nathan Cumberland and his young family. Despite his injuries, through his work with the Defence Medical Welfare Service charity, Nathan has devoted his life outside the armed forces to supporting older and elderly veterans who are often less likely to seek help for themselves than their younger counterparts. With tips on how to create a beautiful woodland themed outdoor space, Alan, Katie Rushworth, Frances Tophill and David Domoney take on Nathan's inaccessible plot and transform it into a show-stopping family garden

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott prepares dishes inspired by school dinners, including shepherd's pie with minted peas, mac and cheese with bacon and leeks, and jam and coconut sponge with custard. He is joined by guests Kimberly Wyatt, who whips up a cheese and bean tortilla, and Juliet Sear, who puts her own spin on a classic school dessert with her cornflake tart

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent

Eight acts perform in each semi-final, with two finalists selected by the judges and the votes of viewers

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Paul, Arlene, John and Dolores pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts the supermarket-based game show, inviting three pairs of shoppers to 'go wild in the aisles' as they compete in the ultimate supermarket challenge to win a £3,000 prize. New quiz rounds feature alongside the legendary trolley dashes and the most famous pastel jumpers on television

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts as comedian Joe Lycett, Pussycat Doll Melody Thornton and retired cricket commentator Henry Blofeld take part in the game show, in which they must answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping counters down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - with the highest scorer playing for the £20,000 jackpot for their chosen charity

programme.title: Family Fortunes
programme.desc: Gino D'Acampo hosts this revamped version of the popular game show, which sees two families trying to guess the answers to survey-based questions posed to 100 people. The family who score the most points go through to the final round, where they play for the a £30,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Singapore Grip
programme.desc: The Blacketts intensify their campaign to secure Matthew's allegiances and Walter remains unyielding, despite the Japanese bombing Singapore. As Vera nurses Matthew through his fevered delirium, the Major is warned not to trust her. Sylvia's brother Charlie rejoins his regiment and Ehrendorf volunteers, and Matthew makes a momentous decision

programme.title: In Cold Blood
programme.desc: In the 1970s, a new treatment for haemophilia known as Factor VIII was prescribed on the NHS. It infected more than 1,300 people with HIV and more than 4,000 people with Hepatitis C. As a public inquiry into the scandal reopens, this investigative feature-length documentary for ITV's Bafta-winning Exposure strand offers a window into a tragedy whose scale and impact has been relatively understated in Britain

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. This time, Virgin Atlantic's punctuality record is put in jeopardy by a captain's bottle of water, and there is love, actually in the air

programme.title: Motorsport UK
programme.desc: Richard John Neil action from Brands Hatch, featuring round five of the Ginetta GT4 Supercup and round six of the Ginetta Junior Championship

programme.title: British Superbike Championship Highlights
programme.desc: James Haydon presents highlights of all three races in the fourth round of the campaign from Snetterton. Glenn Irwin of Honda Racing was top of the standings after the opening three races and was looking to extend his lead

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Celebrity interviews and studio discussion from a female perspective

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Tenable

programme.title: Coronation Street

programme.title: Honour
programme.desc: When Banaz Mahmod's boyfriend reports her missing, DCI Caroline Goode discovers the missing 20-year-old had been to the police five times to report threats to life, even presenting a list of people wanting to kill her for shaming her family. Fact-based drama, starring Keeley Hawes

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: London Zoo: An Extraordinary Year
programme.desc: In March, ZSL London Zoo and its sister zoo Whipsnade fell silent as potential visitors stayed away. This two-part documentary reveals how a dedicated skeleton staff continued to care for the 20,000 animals in Regent's Park and the Dunstable Downs in Bedfordshire. Gemma and Meg must find a way to ensure that the pregnancy care for an endangered okapi isn't thrown off track by the lack of staff. Jamie continues the arachnid breeding programme, introducing a couple of huntsman spiders to each other - but it's a fraught business that could end in love-making or cannibalism

programme.title: Honour
programme.desc: When Banaz Mahmod's boyfriend reports her missing, DCI Caroline Goode discovers the missing 20-year-old had been to the police five times to report threats to life, even presenting a list of people wanting to kill her for shaming her family. Fact-based drama, starring Keeley Hawes

programme.title: Parveen's Indian Kitchen
programme.desc: Parveen Ashraf prepares spiced scrambled eggs and chai, a quick saag aloo, and homemade naans. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: London Zoo: An Extraordinary Year
programme.desc: In March, ZSL London Zoo and its sister zoo Whipsnade fell silent as potential visitors stayed away. This two-part documentary reveals how a dedicated skeleton staff continued to care for the 20,000 animals in Regent's Park and the Dunstable Downs in Bedfordshire. Gemma and Meg must find a way to ensure that the pregnancy care for an endangered okapi isn't thrown off track by the lack of staff. Jamie continues the arachnid breeding programme, introducing a couple of huntsman spiders to each other - but it's a fraught business that could end in love-making or cannibalism

programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Community spirit in the village of Morton Fendle is dealt a blow by the collapse of a craft centre in which many locals had invested - and Alan Hollingsworth, the businessman behind the project, has become a public hate figure. Barnaby investigates when Hollingsworth's wife disappears, and seems to have been kidnapped. However, when Hollingsworth is subsequently murdered and his wife reappears safe and sound, he suspects an elaborate scheme. Roger Allam guest stars

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth contemplates retiring while on a trip to the Lake District, but shelves his plans when Hastings meets a woman whose collection of valuable miniature portraits has been stolen. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The murder of a babysitter throws up a number of questions for Lewis and Hathaway - was she the intended victim or was the killer targeting her employers? Was the crime calculated or opportunistic? And why was she elaborately tied up after being murdered? As the detectives dig for clues, they are led into a world of suburban swinging and fetish photography, far removed from the Oxford they know. Gary Kemp (The Krays), Lucy Cohu (The Awakening), Ciaran McMenamin (Primeval) and Georgia Taylor (Casualty) guest star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Spurred into action by the discovery of a woman's body in Midsomer Worthy's Raven's Wood, Barnaby and Troy re-examine three similar unsolved murders in the same village nine years previously, hoping to get a lead before the killer strikes again. As they dig deeper into the clandestine world of the community, they find themselves embroiled in a sinister web of deceit, violence and mystery. Guest starring Phyllis Logan, and featuring Toby Jones' first appearance in a recurring role as pathologist Dan Peterson

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Travellers descend on the sleepy village of Martyr Warren, antagonising local magistrate Hector Bridges, whose subsequent death by shooting brings matters to a head in dramatic fashion. Barnaby and Troy's investigation uncovers the victim has a history with one of the travellers - but revelations that he terrorised his family suggest that the killer may be closer to home

programme.title: Van Der Valk
programme.desc: Marc Warren steps into the shoes of Barry Foster to take on the role of Dutch detective Van der Valk in this updated adaptation of the Amsterdam-based crime drama. Two seemingly unrelated men are found murdered in different parts of the city on the same day. Are the deaths connected, and if so, could there be a link to the disappearance of a student activist? Commissaris Van der Valk must uncover the truth while also dealing with an over-eager rookie cop joining his tight-knit team. Maimie McCoy, Luke Allen-Gale and Elliot Barnes Worrell co-star

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. A professional hitman bursts into the luxurious family home of accountant Keith Rothwell and shoots him in cold blood. Banks is instantly struck by the strange reactions of the victim's family and evidence comes to light that Rothwell has been living a secret double life. Now investigating the murder of a victim with two identities, Banks and Morton discover a possible link to a major money-laundering operation and are plunged into a high-profile fraud investigation with political ramifications

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. With Pamela Jefferies in a coma following her vicious attack, Banks becomes more determined to find Rothwell's killer. Convinced a vital clue is staring him in the face, he resolves to learn more about Keith Rothwell and, against orders, questions MP Martin Fleming, putting him on a collision course with rival DCI Burgess. Meanwhile, the return of Tom Rothwell to the UK presents Helen with another suspect and an opportunity to put pressure on his family. With Patrick Baladi

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. Frustrated that a number of cases have been knocked back by the Crown Prosecution Service, Banks and the team are called on to investigate the murder of teenager Ellie Clayton, daughter of a high-profile internet entrepreneur. The victim's background, together with the suggestion she may have been raped, means the incident is soon the subject of much media speculation, and strong evidence implicates a theatre director who ran a drama workshop the girl attended on the night she died. The detective believes he has found his man, but Morton fears her colleague isn't seeing the bigger picture

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A wealthy banker goes missing while walking from his country house to the village post office. Poirot, who has acquired a parrot and developed a taste for conjuring tricks, bets the baffled Inspector Japp £5 he can solve the mystery without leaving his own apartment. David Suchet stars

programme.desc: Sid is very surprised to find a well under his house. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James as the head of the family, struggling to keep his wife and children in order

Mystery, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey, Richard Briers and Judy Parfitt

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A village of Apern Tallow is rocked by a series of ghostly occurrences that begins with the inexplicable slashing of a painting in the local museum. Barnaby investigates, shadowed by Cully's actor boyfriend, who is preparing for a role as a police officer - but the mysterious events take a far darker turn when a man is found dead on the grave of his ancestor. Prunella Scales guest stars

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A magician's assistant drops dead on stage, poisoned by the toxins from a tropical frog. While Barnaby and Jones begin their investigations, tempers flare in the local community between Ernest, a firm believer in a mystical cult, and his former friend Aloysius - whose background in the jungles of South America makes him the prime suspect for the murder. Starring John Nettles, Ronald Pickup and Stuart Wilson

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A masterpiece by landscape artist Henry Hogson causes a stir when it is auctioned for £400,000 after a fierce bidding war - and hours later, its former owner is tortured and killed. When a Hogson work is then stolen, Barnaby and Jones research the Midsomer painter with the help of art teacher Matilda Simms. They soon uncover evidence of forgery - but the killer is still at large. Guest starring David Bamber and Susannah Harker, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Inside the Crown: Secrets of the Royals
programme.desc: Documentary revealing how Queen Elizabeth II has mastered the art of being a monarch, both home and abroad, over her record-breaking 68-year reign. She is seen meeting world leaders such as Ronald Reagan, Mikhail Gorbachev and Donald Trump - although not all encounters go well, and the film also looks at the Queen's commitment to the Commonwealth, a personal project that has seen the old Empire transformed

programme.title: Kate & Koji
programme.desc: Kate, the owner of an old-fashioned seaside cafe forms an unlikely friendship with Koji, an African asylum seeker. When Kate finds out that her new acquaintance is a doctor but he is prohibited from working whilst seeking asylum, she hatches a plan that may be mutually beneficial to both involved. Comedy, starring Brenda Blethyn and Jimmy Akingbola

programme.title: Kate & Koji
programme.desc: Six-part series following Kate, a working-class woman who runs an old-fashioned cafe in a seaside town, as she develops a strong, if sometimes volatile, friendship with an asylum-seeking African doctor, Koji. With Kate and Koji's undercover clinic in full swing, the last thing they need is a visit from Dr Radwan. Medium keeps Koji calm, and Kate cooks up a surefire way to get back at an adversary. Comedy, starring Brenda Blethyn, Jimmy Akingbola, Blake Harrison, Barbara Flynn and Victor McGuire

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. The collapse of Owen Pierce's trial has left the case wide open and there's still a killer on the loose, but Banks remains determined to build a watertight legal case against his prime suspect. As the investigation continues, Morton and Jackman uncover promising new leads focusing on Ellie's ex-boyfriend Tyler, but just as they seem to be getting close to the truth, another schoolgirl is murdered, putting Pierce in the spotlight once again

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: The crime drama starring Stephen Tompkinson returns with the first of a two-part story. A man and woman claiming to be social workers visit a mother and tell her they need to take her son away. However, when they fail to bring him back, it transpires that the pair are not known by the authorities. Banks is drawn into a full-scale search for the missing boy, but new evidence and inconsistent witness statements cause the detective to question the truth of what he has been told. Co-starring Andrea Lowe, Caroline Catz, Christine Bottomley and John Lightbody

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates the murder of her old friend Father Gorman, using a list of names sent by the priest moments before his death to bring the guilty party to justice. Following a trail of clues to London, Marple is drawn to the Pale Horse Inn - where she discovers a sinister world of occult practices. Drama, starring Julia McKenzie in the title role, with Lynda Baron, Pauline Collins, Nicholas Parsons and Neil Pearson

programme.desc: Eagle-eyed George sees an investment opportunity when Mildred suggests their dog should have puppies. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack is taken hostage while interviewing a suspect, but somehow manages to talk his way out of trouble - quite an achievement for a man whose lack of tact is legendary. Having experienced what it's like to look down a gun barrel, he barges into a nearby Army camp where investigators are trying to solve a soldier's murder, but finds his help isn't appreciated. With David Jason, Bruce Alexander, Sam Kelly, Nicholas Jones and Saira Todd

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Bellamy tries to impress Miller by going it alone, while the arrival of tax inspector Derek Ford gets the residents of Aidensfield panicked. Vernon bears the brunt of Ford's wrath when his dodgy dealings and poor record-keeping are exposed, convincing the wheeler dealer the only way to avoid trouble is to fake his own suicide. Rural drama, guest starring James Weaver, with Mark Jordon, Geoffrey Hughes and John Duttine

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Robert feels sad as Andy is leaving. Chris is still fuming with Kim for making him look a fool. Zoe meets Sophie's mother

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Betty confronts Zoe about her rent increase, Sophie slaps Jan, and Charlie sells her car as she makes plans to leave Emmerdale

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Bill and Jim have trouble when their van breaks down. Deirdre is thrilled when Tracy visits, but she has a shock in store for her mum. Jim stops the cheque he gives to Liz

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim buys a new van. Ken and Deirdre are delighted when Tracy tells them she is getting married in Weatherfield. Bill forces Jim to tell Liz about stopping the cheque

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Troy is playing in an annual cricket match between Midsomer Worthy and Fletchers Cross when tragedy strikes. The wife of one of the team captains, a wealthy local landowner, is found battered to death with a cricket bat in a quarry. As Barnaby and Troy investigate, they discover no end of people with a grudge against the victim's husband - who may have been complicit in some other suspicious deaths. Guest starring Imelda Staunton and Robert Hardy

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A local gigolo is murdered, forcing Jack to face the uncomfortable fact that some women are willing to pay for sex - and triggering an investigation that takes him into the seedy world of male escorts and their clientele. David Jason stars as the old-fashioned copper, with Isla Blair, Russell Hunter, Joanna David and Martin Jarvis

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When the sleuth's dentist is found dead just minutes after he leaves his surgery, Chief Inspector Japp believes it to be suicide - but Poirot has reason to think otherwise and embarks on his own investigation. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. The detective now has a murder inquiry on his hands as the body found on the moors is identified as 11-year-old Andre Petri. With Kyle Heath still missing, Banks divides his team to search for a connection between the cases, and when post-mortem examinations show Andre was killed by a heroin overdose, it appears the two boys are linked by the illegal drug trade. Starring Stephen Tompkinson, with Andrea Lowe, Caroline Catz, Christine Bottomley and John Lightbody

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Julia McKenzie returns as the sleuthing spinster in the first of three murder mysteries. While staying at a hotel in the Caribbean, Miss Marple investigates the death of a fellow guest, refusing to believe he died of a heart attack. With the help of a business tycoon she sets about finding the real cause of the man's demise, and is soon drawn into a web of deceit and dark magic. Adapted by Charlie Higson and co-starring Antony Sher, Robert Webb, Montserrat Lombard, Daniel Rigby, Hermione Norris, MyAnna Buring, Warren Brown and Oliver Ford Davies

programme.desc: A local gigolo is murdered, forcing Jack to face the uncomfortable fact that some women are willing to pay for sex - and triggering an investigation that takes him into the seedy world of male escorts and their clientele. David Jason stars as the old-fashioned copper, with Isla Blair, Russell Hunter, Joanna David and Martin Jarvis

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean worries about strange noises coming from next door. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James as the head of the family, struggling to keep his wife and children in tow

programme.title: Carry On Cruising (1962)
programme.desc: The captain of a cruise ship bound for the Mediterranean is less than impressed when five of his regular shipmates are replaced at the last minute. Despite their best efforts to earn his respect and an eagerness to please, the new recruits seem doomed to cause chaos whatever they do. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Liz Fraser and Kenneth Connor

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Community spirit in the village of Morton Fendle is dealt a blow by the collapse of a craft centre in which many locals had invested - and Alan Hollingsworth, the businessman behind the project, has become a public hate figure. Barnaby investigates when Hollingsworth's wife disappears, and seems to have been kidnapped. However, when Hollingsworth is subsequently murdered and his wife reappears safe and sound, he suspects an elaborate scheme. Roger Allam guest stars

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth contemplates retiring while on a trip to the Lake District, but shelves his plans when Hastings meets a woman whose collection of valuable miniature portraits has been stolen. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The murder of a babysitter throws up a number of questions for Lewis and Hathaway - was she the intended victim or was the killer targeting her employers? Was the crime calculated or opportunistic? And why was she elaborately tied up after being murdered? As the detectives dig for clues, they are led into a world of suburban swinging and fetish photography, far removed from the Oxford they know. Gary Kemp (The Krays), Lucy Cohu (The Awakening), Ciaran McMenamin (Primeval) and Georgia Taylor (Casualty) guest star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Spurred into action by the discovery of a woman's body in Midsomer Worthy's Raven's Wood, Barnaby and Troy re-examine three similar unsolved murders in the same village nine years previously, hoping to get a lead before the killer strikes again. As they dig deeper into the clandestine world of the community, they find themselves embroiled in a sinister web of deceit, violence and mystery. Guest starring Phyllis Logan, and featuring Toby Jones' first appearance in a recurring role as pathologist Dan Peterson

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Travellers descend on the sleepy village of Martyr Warren, antagonising local magistrate Hector Bridges, whose subsequent death by shooting brings matters to a head in dramatic fashion. Barnaby and Troy's investigation uncovers the victim has a history with one of the travellers - but revelations that he terrorised his family suggest that the killer may be closer to home

programme.title: Van Der Valk
programme.desc: Marc Warren steps into the shoes of Barry Foster to take on the role of Dutch detective Van der Valk in this updated adaptation of the Amsterdam-based crime drama. Two seemingly unrelated men are found murdered in different parts of the city on the same day. Are the deaths connected, and if so, could there be a link to the disappearance of a student activist? Commissaris Van der Valk must uncover the truth while also dealing with an over-eager rookie cop joining his tight-knit team. Maimie McCoy, Luke Allen-Gale and Elliot Barnes Worrell co-star

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. A professional hitman bursts into the luxurious family home of accountant Keith Rothwell and shoots him in cold blood. Banks is instantly struck by the strange reactions of the victim's family and evidence comes to light that Rothwell has been living a secret double life. Now investigating the murder of a victim with two identities, Banks and Morton discover a possible link to a major money-laundering operation and are plunged into a high-profile fraud investigation with political ramifications

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. With Pamela Jefferies in a coma following her vicious attack, Banks becomes more determined to find Rothwell's killer. Convinced a vital clue is staring him in the face, he resolves to learn more about Keith Rothwell and, against orders, questions MP Martin Fleming, putting him on a collision course with rival DCI Burgess. Meanwhile, the return of Tom Rothwell to the UK presents Helen with another suspect and an opportunity to put pressure on his family. With Patrick Baladi

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. Frustrated that a number of cases have been knocked back by the Crown Prosecution Service, Banks and the team are called on to investigate the murder of teenager Ellie Clayton, daughter of a high-profile internet entrepreneur. The victim's background, together with the suggestion she may have been raped, means the incident is soon the subject of much media speculation, and strong evidence implicates a theatre director who ran a drama workshop the girl attended on the night she died. The detective believes he has found his man, but Morton fears her colleague isn't seeing the bigger picture

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A wealthy banker goes missing while walking from his country house to the village post office. Poirot, who has acquired a parrot and developed a taste for conjuring tricks, bets the baffled Inspector Japp £5 he can solve the mystery without leaving his own apartment. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid is very surprised to find a well under his house. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James as the head of the family, struggling to keep his wife and children in order
